titleStmt.title: Mr. Lauchlin Currie, Administrative Assistant to President Roosevelt, to the Secretary of State

head.hi: Mr. Lauchlin Currie, Administrative Assistant to President Roosevelt, to the Secretary of State
head.note: Forwarded on May 5 to the Adviser on Political Relations (Hornbeck) with a request for transmission to the Secretary of State.

div.p: Memorandum for the Secretary of State:

In accordance with your suggestion, I discussed this matter with Dr. Hornbeck. With reference to communications by private code between the President and Chiang Kai-shek, we decided that although it would be preferable to have all messages go through regular diplomatic channels, still, in this instance, it seemed advisable to reply to Chiang Kai-shek’s message in his code through commercial channels. Otherwise, he might feel rebuffed and embarrassed. Dr. Hornbeck
assisted in the drafting of the attached message Supra. which was coded in the State Department’s code room and approved by the President. If there should be any further messages, I shall show them to you immediately and consult you on any replies.

With reference to my own communications with Major McHugh, Naval Attaché at Chungking, on matters pertaining to lease-lend aid for China, we decided that for the time being I might continue to use naval radio, but that messages to and from should be immediately communicated to you at this end, and to Ambassador GaussClarence E. Gauss, appointed Ambassador to China to succeed Nelson Trusler Johnson. at the other. I think it would be desirable for you to inform the Ambassador of this arrangement if you approve.
